cards.hook: Anthropic's Mythos model has people worried
cards.caption: Anthropic's new Mythos model already has ex-founders raising alarms on CBS. When the builders get nervous, you pay attention. This is the signal my feed pulls before the noise buries it.
cards.stance: The people closest to frontier models are the most scared of them.

cards.hook: Microsoft is building AI to undercut OpenAI
cards.caption: Microsoft is quietly shipping in-house models to undercut its own partner OpenAI. Your cheapest AI provider might soon be the one funding the competition. Caught this before the timeline did.
cards.stance: Microsoft doesn't need OpenAI anymore — it needs a cheaper OpenAI.

cards.hook: Google I/O 2026: Search rebuilt, Gemini goes bigger
cards.caption: Google just rebuilt Search around AI and pushed Gemini bigger at I/O 2026. If you search or build, your defaults are about to change. The stuff my feed keeps so you skip the keynote.

cards.hook: Anthropic says its new model is too dangerous to release
cards.caption: Anthropic says Mythos is too dangerous to ship freely — so it's teaming with rivals to secure it. A lab holding back its own model is the story of the year. Found before the timeline.
cards.stance: The scariest AI headline isn't a leak — it's a lab refusing to release.

cards.hook: Model launches used to be events. Now they're rolling.
cards.caption: Frontier labs quietly killed the big launch — models now ship continuously, not as events. If you still wait for release day, you're already behind. The shift my feed caught early.
cards.stance: The era of the AI keynote is over — updates arrive silently now.

cards.hook: Google's AI endgame at I/O 2026
Anthropic built AI too risky to release publicly
cards.caption: ABC News confirms it: Anthropic built a model it deems too dangerous to release. When a lab won't ship its best work, that tells you where we are. Caught before the timeline.

cards.hook: Anthropic's Mythos is so powerful it's under limited test only
cards.caption: Anthropic is testing Mythos on a tiny leash — too powerful for a full release. The most capable model of the year is the one you can't touch yet. The stuff my feed keeps.

cards.hook: Anthropic just delayed a Claude model over safety
cards.caption: Anthropic just delayed "Claude Mythos" over security concerns. A frontier lab hitting pause is the signal you actually want to track. Found before the timeline caught it.
cards.stance: A lab that ships slower on purpose is the one worth trusting.

cards.hook: Meta shipped its most powerful model yet: Muse Spark
cards.caption: Meta just dropped Muse Spark, its strongest AI model and the first product from Alexandr Wang's new lab. The model race just got a new frontrunner. The stuff my feed keeps.

cards.hook: OpenAI's GPT-5.2 is built to replace your workflow
cards.caption: GPT-5.2 is out: better spreadsheets, slides, code, and image reads in one model. If it does your busywork, that changes your day, not just a benchmark. Found before the timeline.
cards.stance: The next model war isn't about IQ, it's about who kills your busywork first.

cards.hook: Meta's Muse Spark is aimed straight at ChatGPT
cards.caption: Meta unveils Muse Spark to rival the top chatbots, the first in a new line of LLMs. Duplicate of the bigger Muse Spark drop, so I keep the stronger card. The stuff my feed keeps.

cards.hook: The AI news you missed this week
cards.caption: A weekly AI roundup teasing Microsoft's plan. Too vague, no real claim, pure clickbait wrapper. Rejecting: my feed keeps the source drops, not the recap channels.

cards.hook: Microsoft just dropped 7 in-house AI models
cards.caption: Microsoft revealed seven of its own models, IQ, Scout, MDASH, Majorana 2, going full-stack instead of leaning on OpenAI. The dependency era is ending. Found before the timeline.
cards.stance: Microsoft building its own models is the quiet end of the OpenAI marriage.

cards.hook: Microsoft just built 7 of its own AI models
cards.caption: Microsoft announces a family of seven in-house models across image, voice, and more. Direct from the source, not a recap channel. The stuff my feed keeps before it trends.

cards.hook: Gemma 4 12B fuses vision, audio, and text in one pass
cards.caption: Google DeepMind's Gemma 4 12B ditches the three-model stack and processes images, sound, and text together. Fewer moving parts, cheaper to run. Found before the timeline.
cards.stance: One unified model beats three specialists once the plumbing stops fighting itself.

cards.hook: Frontier AI labs quietly killed the release milestone
cards.caption:
Frontier AI labs quietly killed the release milestone.

2023-24 had clear moments: GPT-4, Claude 3, Gemini 1.5. Mid-2025 flipped to silent rolling updates. The stuff my feed keeps.
cards.stance: Version numbers are dead; the model you used yesterday isn't the one you're using now.

cards.hook: Anthropic's own report is a stark warning about Claude
cards.caption:
Anthropic issued a stark warning about its own model.

CBS pulled in an ex-AI-founder to break down the Claude Mythos report. When the lab that builds it sounds the alarm, you read it. Found before the timeline.

cards.hook: Microsoft's AI CEO: build superintelligence that won't replace you
cards.caption:
Microsoft's AI CEO just unveiled 7 new models with one rule: don't replace people.

Mustafa Suleyman calls it Humanist Superintelligence at Build 2026. A direct shot at the everyone-gets-automated crowd. The stuff my feed keeps.
cards.stance: "Humanist superintelligence" is a positioning move as much as a mission.

cards.hook: GPT-5.6 Sol just ended open AI competition
The fastest-growing AI project right now is an eval suite
cards.caption:
The fastest-growing AI project this week isn't a model.

It's benchflow-ai's "awesome-evals" — devs care more about testing agents than shipping them. Tells you where the field is actually heading. Found before the timeline.
cards.stance: The next AI gold rush is evaluation, not generation.

cards.hook: awesome-evals is still the most active AI repo
Community-driven AI agent toolkits keep surging
A dev built a stress-test arena for AI shopping agents
cards.caption:
Someone built a fake marketplace just to attack AI shopping agents.

Super AI Markets stress-tests how autonomous buyers handle adversarial stores. Agentic commerce has a security hole nobody's watching. The stuff my feed keeps.
cards.stance: Your future AI shopping agent is a fresh attack surface, and almost no one is red-teaming it.

cards.hook: The fastest-growing AI repos this week aren't the ones you starred
cards.caption: The fastest-growing AI repos this week aren't the ones you starred. Awesome-evals is quietly blowing up while everyone chases models. This is the kind of signal my feed keeps.
cards.stance: Eval tooling is the real AI infra story, not another model drop.

cards.hook: Multi-agent systems are eating this week's fastest-growing AI repos
cards.caption: Multi-agent systems are eating this week's fastest-growing AI repos. Benchflow-ai/awesome-evals is trending hard. Near-duplicate of yesterday's roundup, so I'd skip this one.

cards.hook: Tencent just shipped Hunyuan 3 with a doc-generating agent built in
cards.caption: Tencent just shipped Hunyuan 3 with a doc-generating agent built in. Upgraded reasoning, and the West barely noticed. Chinese labs move fast, my feed catches it before the timeline.
cards.stance: The frontier race isn't OpenAI vs Anthropic. It's US labs vs Chinese labs you never hear about.

cards.caption: Random trader hot take pegging BTC price to AI model release schedules. No real signal, just crypto-Twitter noise citing unverifiable Fable and GPT-Sol launches.
Vague self-promo about a 'ProbePrompt' idea with no concrete result or method. Reads as personal musing, not a signal worth reposting for a tech audience.

cards.hook: Anthropic built a model so powerful they only ran a 'very limited test'
cards.caption: Anthropic built a model so powerful they only ran a 'very limited test.' It's called Mythos, and the safety concerns are the whole story. This is what my feed surfaces before it trends.
cards.stance: When a lab throttles its own launch, that tells you more than any benchmark.

cards.hook: Anthropic just delayed Claude Mythos over security concerns
cards.caption: Anthropic just delayed Claude Mythos, calling it not ready for the public. A frontier lab hitting the brakes is rare, and it matters. Caught this before the timeline did.
cards.stance: The scariest AI news isn't a launch. It's a launch getting paused.

cards.hook: Meta's new Muse Spark is the first drop from Alexandr Wang's lab
cards.caption: Meta's Muse Spark just landed, the first product from Alexandr Wang's new AI org. Their most powerful model yet, per CNBC. The org shakeup is the real signal here, my feed flagged it.
cards.stance: Meta buying Scale's founder was the play. Muse Spark is the receipt.
